reasoning: Diethyl ether-boron trifluoride (BF3·OEt2) is a Lewis acid-base complex where boron trifluoride coordinates with diethyl ether. It contains a boron atom bonded to organic/heteroatom groups, classifying it as an organoboron compound; however, it also contains fluorine and is technically a coordination complex that doesn't fit neatly into the taxonomy.
